author: Pardeep K.
pros: Axosoft is self explanatory Easy to create projects back logs
cons: Won't allow to perform multi level nesting

review_title: Axosoft as Support Ticket Management tool? yes.
description: Our experience with Axosoft has been very good. Our business had the tool as a Project Management tool, and, as we grew, we were in need of a tool to track our customer support tickets. This did the trick.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I have found the reporting from Axosoft to be very strong. The ability to export so much data to then manipulate in Excel or similar was fantastic.
cons: There are a few areas of the tool that we found to be difficult to follow due to the UI. This has not been a deal breaker, but simply required for training for new employees.

author: Partha S.
pros: Has decent features, such as Agile board and reporting.
cons: Was very hard to integrate with the other software that our organization was using.

description: Axosoft has improved our bug tracking and support incident tracking by making everything easily accessible and visible.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Easy to configure and easy to use. Many customizations, security options. It's especially nice that you can copy a Support Incident to a Work Ticket. The right-click menu makes everything easily accessible!
cons: The only thing I can think of is that I would like to see time tracking (Estimate and Work Logs) for each Work Type and then the total. We can currently only track one value, so we track our bottleneck, development. QA and Documentation have to enter work logs with 0 hours or it skews our estimate v. actual analysis.

review_title: Could be a bit streamlined but otherwise ok
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Estimates and time budgets are in easy view. I also like that it gives you options to change views between KANBAN and list view, and also how easy you can plan and organize sprints. It's also nice being able to integrate with slack to keep everything in one place and somewhat seamless.
cons: The personal user interface is a bit cluttered and could be streamlined. It's not as intuitive as it could be, especially when trying to keep track of your own dashboard and workspace. The "work Items" section could also be a little cleaner and have some of the subsections consolidated.
author_industry: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Matthew W.
pros: It allowed me to separate my ticket bucket how I liked.
cons: It would often crash, the bugs would sometimes render it inoperable. Sometimes it gets stuck loading. Its layout is very dated and unresponsive

description: We started with Axosoft when it was called OnTime. It worked great for us as a smaller organization for planning, tracking, and releasing software. Functionality and pricing is very competitive. The UI is intuitive and offers lots of glaceable and reportable information.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Great for SCRUM and defect tracking. Easy to plan iteration. Intuitive UI.
cons: While documentation was pretty good, there isn't as much of a user community compared to other solutions.

description: Overall, I came into Axosoft as a user after it was set up. I was responsible for transitioning the backlog from Axosoft to another product that I chose. That process wasn't too cumbersome. For the price of Axosoft, I expected better features.
author: Deborah G.
pros: Cloud based software that provided a fairly good user interface. Our team members did not have many issues using the software.
cons: Axosoft did not provide as much visibility into the projects and releases as I would have liked. Tasks/Issues seemed to get lost. Time tracking was also lacking.

description: A method of cataloging any issues experienced using a new type of software
author: Leigh L.
pros: A "windows based" type software which looks reassuringly professional and is fairly straightforward to use, even for the uninitiated novice
cons: It is not easy to filter the columns necessarily needed for specific business areas / business interests so that certain types of information can be located and tracked
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: What I liked most about this software is the amount of features that it has. The email implementation is very easy and creating groups and users is a breeze.
cons: Is a little "cluttery " from the get-go. It offers the option to simplify and customize the experience so is not a big deal. It might take a little to get use to.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: tool to share happiness? start here
author: Damaris C.
pros: It is the tool that gives more meaning to the Scrum Framework, it is very easy to use, it has all the functions for the ceremonies and the burndown chat is wonderful.
cons: The Organizational structure (Project organizational levels) of the projects in the tool does not allow editing in terms of eliminating levels, it allows only to edit the name / version .. it is quite rigid

author: Valentina G.
pros: 1. One of the best software i have used for management. 2. It is incredibly complete; everything you can think about management, Axosoft has it.
cons: 1. It is not the friendliest aesthetic you can find, the letters are small and the colors are very dark. 2. It is not a software for beginners, Axosoft has a way of management that not everyone can use it.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: We use Axosoft to track new development efforts and bug fixes
author: Rachel F.
pros: It is very customizeable and has great filtering tools. It's easy to make sure all of the necessary details are included in each item.
cons: From time to time I will accidentally make a change that auto-saves, when I didn't mean to. The undo function fixes this pretty easily though.

author: Tim L.
pros: Have when this software was first developed it was cutting edge and quite easy to use. Users still love this software.
cons: Like many programs, when purchased by a larger competitor, the focus on development becomes less important and as in this case it has fallen behind its competitors.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Longtime Axosoft User, love the product. Best one we've bought in years!
description: I like that we can email tickets into the system. I like that I can add custom fields with custom rules to force my people to use forms a specific way. I like that we can create project folders to track what we're working on. I like the new PM functionality. I like the Workflow Steps. I like the Portal plug-in for having customers submit and view tickets. In fact I like everything. Time tracking is very powerful for employee tracking and for R&D write offs. This is the best bang for the buck our company has got since acquiring the product in 2007. I dislike the lost dashboard functionality from converting to the Web. The reporting isn't nearly as powerful now in my opinion. Simple dashboards are great, but we used to have lots of additional widget types that I used. Releases/Projects. My users habitually put things in the wrong place. It requires me to go back and move things around all the time. The Portal works but it's not a nice fancy looking interface for our customers to interface with. We don't use the other integrations. This is a great tool for a QA manager who knows what they want. I have this configured so that my weekly management reports are saved views. I literally can using the Snipping Tool and cut out a screenshot for all my report needs.
author: david Y.
pros: I love the flexibility and the simplicity. This tool was designed as a defect tracking tool and it does that very well. The added functionality over the years have been solid additions to a nicely developed base.
cons: The reporting tools are a bit on the weak side. It's not just that the precanned reports aren't that good, it's that building new ones requires a separate software utility. Also, a lot of my end users have issues with the combination of filtering on the left and the different record types. I've been thinking about updating our design and putting all record types on one tab with a custom "Type" flag to eliminate the needs to go back and forth, but they came out with an All Items tab, so hopefully that'll help.

description: We are solving company wide project management. We use it to visualize where time is spent for each employee and to determine how long it takes for project to be complete. This gives us insight to plan better in the future by improving our estimates on how long things take.
author: Kyle J.
pros: We use Axosoft for managing team projects utilizing many features of the product such as creating customizable categories for work that we can further break down into what we call sprints (work week). Axosoft does a great job at helping understand where time is most being spent by having employees log time on each task/defect/feature which generates burn down charts to see if you are hitting your target release time.
cons: Occasionally the css seems to fail to load and the screen just one big mess and you have to restart the server to fix the issue. There are some bugs with trying to delete cards where it wont let you so we just give them a status as declined. Also, it is missing some workflow logic for testing, we have to put all our testing logic in a text editor box and it would be great if these could be be sub-tasks that can be checked off when completed.

review_title: Easy to use tool that will support Agile develomnet
description: We use mainly axosoft for managing our software development agile process and we have it integrated with our time tracking/billing system
author: Mauricio M.
pros: Easy of use, tracking of time, easy for creation of tasks, bugs and features. It has an API that can be integrated with other managements systems
cons: Sometimes it is difficult to find items with the search button. And newly created items can disappear easily because of filters

review_title: This software has many pros and few cons. The learning curve is a little high but great once learned
description: The ability to track projects and track another users progress on their projects are very beneficial. Tracking different cards and being able to categorize them and prepare for releases saves a lot of time for the managers of the project. Understanding where other users are at in their section of a project lets other users know where they are in their own project and if they need to gather more resources for their part.
author: Justin K.
pros: There are a lot of feature that are included in Axosoft. The ability to track projects and sprints are great. The ability to find card is also very easy and simple. The categorization is very streamlined once it has been learned by the team.
cons: The page auto refreshes after entering information into certain fields and resets the cursor to the previous field which can cause some problems. There is a steep learning curve to learning everything about this software.

review_title: Great tool for multi projec tracking
description: We started off years ago with the free bug tracker and then they carried us forward into the more full experience and a great price. Up time has been great and rarely is there ever a problem. Over all rock solid.
author: Adam F.
pros: Ability to organize tasks by varying projects, assign releases, and customize to fit our team.
cons: Just a few minor points here and there of how things work. But overall you can work around those things.

review_title: End user. I both submit requests for work to be done and am assigned work through Axosoft.
author: Cormac S.
pros: Not much. I guess it is responsive when you click around it. There is no lag or slowness when trying to navigate through the crowded screens.
cons: The interface is crowded and it can be difficult knowing right where to click immediately. Everything's divided up into boxes that move different places. It seems like the box I need next is always squished over to the side somewhere instead of easily accessible. Also, the search function is not intuitive at all. The methods for filtering results are difficult to navigate and often inadequate for what I need to display.

description: We are an Agile shop and rely heavily on Axosoft (formerly OnTime) for our Sprint planning and execution. It's a great product! It makes it easy to do Sprint planning and to track process. All of our project managers use Axosoft except one, and there is a huge difference in the stand-ups where Axosoft is up on the screen versus the one project manager that uses spreadsheets. There has been some great progress made in the UI, although it still has a way to go to be fully polished (improved filter management, being on the cloud rather than installed on one of our on-premise servers).
author: David G.
pros: Very Agile-centric
cons: The UI needs improving (filter management, reports, views). Is installed on on-premise server instead of the cloud.

description: I think that we have not yet taken full advantage of this tool, as long as we continue to use it with certainty we will discover more benefits
author: Assaf Y.
pros: It is an excellent software for collaborative work and monitoring in the development and execution of projects under the SCRUM methodology. It offers several options for configuring the panel in such a way as to adapt it to the convenience of the team within the framework of the methodology. It shares information and files, it also tracks the speed of development.
cons: Until now I see little weakness in the tool, maybe what I would recommend is that it offers the possibility of incorporating more sublevels in tasks or stories.

description: Axosoft is a good software piece for project management but I believe improvements are in order. It's not so user friendly, even with the newer updates. Searching is difficult if you need to narrow down the search to a field or multiple fields. Setup is somewhat tedious as well. Otherwise, for a small company, it could be a good resource to use for PM or Dev management.
author: Jeremy G.
pros: Robust for our various and unique needs.
cons: Setup is cumbersome. Searching globally is a bit tedious to make sure you are in the correct tab, otherwise your search is returned invalid.

author: Steven P.
pros: Axosoft is customizable and you can use it to follow the metrics you need. The time-tracker is well developed and progress is updated in real-time . Working on mobile is also a big plus .
cons: There is a learning curve and the program is hard to learn . The UI is especially is confusing and can be hard to navigate .

review_title: One of he best tools for Agile Project Management
description: Its a very good software for Agile projects managenment, it have some tutorials for begginers and if you aret familiar whit agile terms, roles and artifacts the help tool realy help you to understand the software and the agile Project management. Its very good job!
author: Jose V.
pros: At our learning Company we use Axosoft to manage our instructional design process. It was very easy to implement and adapt to our workflow, and so easy when we want to personalyze the units, dashboards, add new data, etc. It helps us yet to trac our velocity, aceleration, and keep on top of the instructional design processes.
cons: We really dont encounter a significative cons, only a few issue was when we change a start date for a history, or to a task once it was began, the dashboard shows it like if we change the effort, but only was a chance on start date
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months
